generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance GantryCartMotor : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "DC motor controlling the cart's linear movement along the gantry beam with precise positional control"
]

instance HoistMotor : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "DC motor managing the vertical container transport mechanism with precise hoisting control"
]

instance CraneElectromagnet : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Electromagnet integrated at the hoist end for precise pick-up and release of containers (pucks) with binary actuation"
]

// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance SwingAngleEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Positional sensor measuring the swing angle of the crane's movable components"
    DTDFVocab:producedData SwingAngleData
]

instance CartPositionEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Positional sensor tracking the linear position of the cart along the gantry beam"
    DTDFVocab:producedData CartPositionData
]

instance HoistPositionEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Positional sensor measuring the vertical position of the hoist mechanism"
    DTDFVocab:producedData HoistPositionData
]

instance CraneVelocitySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Motion sensor (e.g., accelerometer) measuring velocity of crane components"
    DTDFVocab:producedData CraneVelocityData
]

instance CraneAccelerationSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Motion sensor (e.g., gyroscope) measuring acceleration of crane components"
    DTDFVocab:producedData CraneAccelerationData
]

instance ControllerInputSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor capturing controller inputs (e.g., motor commands, PID signals) for validation"
    DTDFVocab:producedData ControllerInputData
]

instance EnvironmentalConditionSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor measuring environmental conditions (e.g., temperature, humidity) affecting crane operation"
    DTDFVocab:producedData EnvironmentalConditionData
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance SwingAngleData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SwingAngleEncoder
]

instance CartPositionData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom CartPositionEncoder
]

instance HoistPositionData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HoistPositionEncoder
]

instance CraneVelocityData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom CraneVelocitySensor
]

instance CraneAccelerationData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom CraneAccelerationSensor
]

instance ControllerInputData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom ControllerInputSensor
]

instance EnvironmentalConditionData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EnvironmentalConditionSensor
]

instance CraneStateData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom CartPositionEncoder, HoistPositionEncoder, CraneVelocitySensor, CraneAccelerationSensor, ControllerInputSensor, EnvironmentalConditionSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance CraneKinematicModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Geometric and kinematic model of the gantry crane including CAD-based representations and movement constraints"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo GantryLibEnabler
]

instance CraneBehavioralModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Behavioral model replicating container movement logic and crane operational dynamics"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo GantryLibEnabler
]

instance HistoricalOperationalData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time-series data storing historical crane operational metrics (position, velocity, actuator currents) for analysis"
    DTDFVocab:fromData CraneStateData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo TimescaleDBEnabler
]

instance RealTimeOperationalData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Continuously collected real-time operational metrics from the physical crane for synchronization"
    DTDFVocab:fromData CraneStateData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo TimescaleDBEnabler
]

instance SimulatedTrajectoryData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Precomputed or optimized trajectories generated by the digital twin for comparison with physical operations"
    DTDFVocab:fromData CraneStateData
]

// C11: Enablers
instance GantryLibEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Python-based module (gantrylib) bundling core functionalities including kinematic modeling and control logic"
    DTDFVocab:enables TrajectoryGeneratorService, OptimalControlService
]

instance TimescaleDBEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Time-series database storing historical and real-time operational metrics with efficient querying capabilities"
    DTDFVocab:enables HistoricalDataLoggingService
]

instance MQTTBrokerEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Eclipse Mosquitto MQTT broker facilitating bidirectional, low-latency data exchange between physical and digital systems"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataTransmissionService
]

instance LoggingApplicationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Python program continuously collecting and forwarding crane state data to TimescaleDB with continuous operation"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataTransmissionService
]

instance SimulationServiceEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Service providing simulation capabilities for trajectory validation and optimization"
    DTDFVocab:enables ContinuousValidationService
]

instance GrafanaEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Real-time dashboard platform for monitoring crane performance, sensor data, and system status"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataVisualizationService
]

instance DockerEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Containerization platform ensuring portability and consistency of digital twin components across environments"
    DTDFVocab:enables DeploymentService
]

// C6: Services
instance TrajectoryGeneratorService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service generating optimized trajectories for crane movement based on kinematic models and historical data"
    DTDFVocab:provides TrajectoryOptimizationInsight
]

instance OptimalControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time control optimization service generating energy-efficient motion commands for crane actuators"
    DTDFVocab:provides ControlOptimizationAction
]

instance HistoricalDataLoggingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service logging operational parameters for post-mortem analysis, fault diagnosis, and performance benchmarking"
    DTDFVocab:provides HistoricalDataInsight
]

instance DataVisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service providing interactive dashboards with CAD-based 3D models and time-series plots for system monitoring"
    DTDFVocab:provides SystemMonitoringInsight
]

instance ContinuousValidationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service implementing real-time synchronization and discrepancy detection between physical and digital crane states"
    DTDFVocab:provides ValidationResultInsight
]

instance FaultDetectionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service using data-driven approaches to identify deviations like sensor failures or mechanical wear in real time"
    DTDFVocab:provides FaultDiagnosisInsight
]

instance DataTransmissionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service managing continuous, low-latency data transmission from physical crane to digital twin via MQTT"
    DTDFVocab:provides DataTransmissionInsight
]

instance DeploymentService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service handling containerized deployment of digital twin components for reproducibility"
    DTDFVocab:provides DeploymentStatusInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance TrajectoryOptimizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Optimized trajectories for energy-efficient crane movement with validated kinematic constraints"
]

instance ControlOptimizationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated generation of control signals for crane actuators based on real-time optimization"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance HistoricalDataInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Time-series operational data enabling performance benchmarking and fault diagnosis"
]

instance SystemMonitoringInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time visualization of crane state, sensor data, and digital-physical alignment for debugging"
]

instance ValidationResultInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Discrepancy detection results between physical crane trajectories and digital twin simulations"
]

instance FaultDiagnosisInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Identified anomalies including sensor failures and mechanical wear with diagnostic recommendations"
]

instance DataTransmissionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Status and quality metrics of continuous data transmission between physical and virtual systems"
]

instance DeploymentStatusInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Containerization and deployment status of digital twin components across environments"
]

// C1: Environment
instance GantryCraneEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Closed-loop, deterministic lab-scale environment for gantry crane operations with predefined kinematic constraints"
    base:contains PhysicalCraneComponent, DigitalTwinComponent
]

instance PhysicalCraneComponent : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Mechanical gantry crane system with movable components (hoist, trolley, bridge) for material handling"
    base:isContainedIn GantryCraneEnvironment
]

instance DigitalTwinComponent : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Digital representation of the gantry crane including geometric and behavioral models for simulation and validation"
    base:isContainedIn GantryCraneEnvironment
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The **Virtual-to-Physical Interaction** in this Digital Twin system is mediated by the **Control Application**, which generates and transmits control commands to the **Physical Crane Interface** (a Python program) to execute movements. The **command types** include trajectory profiles (optimized by the **Trajectory Generator Service**) and direct control signals (e.g., velocity/position setpoints). **Control message formats** are not explicitly defined but are assumed to be structured data packets (e.g., JSON or binary) compatible with the crane’s controllers. **Validation and authentication mechanisms** are not described, but the **Continuous Validation Service** implicitly ensures command integrity by comparing executed trajectories against simulations, triggering alerts via the **Dashboard** if deviations exceed thresholds. **Feedback systems** are bidirectional: the **Physical Crane Interface** relays real-time sensor data back to the **Control Application** and **Logging Application**, while the **Dashboard** provides operator feedback on system performance. **Conflict resolution** is not explicitly addressed, but the **Trajectory Generator Service** ensures optimal trajectories without residual swinging, minimizing physical conflicts. **Command execution confirmation** is inferred through the **Continuous Validation Service**, which validates trajectory execution against simulations and notifies operators of discrepancies."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system exemplars adopt a **lightweight, ad-hoc orchestration approach** rather than a standardized framework. The **GreenhouseDT** exemplifies **self-adaptive behavior** for both the physical greenhouse and its digital twin, implying **closed-loop control** with dynamic adjustments. Orchestration relies on **InfluxDB** (time-series database) for data persistence, **ActiveMQ** (message broker) for event-driven communication, and **virtual machines** for deployment, suggesting a **service-oriented architecture (SOA)** with loosely coupled components. The **Air Quality Management** exemplar uses **AutomationML** for model-driven digital twin engineering, enabling **runtime visualization, simulation, and prediction** as distinct services. Orchestration is implemented via **Microsoft Azure tools**, indicating **cloud-native integration** with potential for **scalable resource management** (e.g., auto-scaling for simulation workloads). The **gantry crane** system leverages the **C4 Model** for architecture visualization, implying a **modular, containerized design** with clear separation of concerns (e.g., physical crane control vs. digital twin logic). No centralized orchestrator is mentioned; instead, **containerization/virtualization** (e.g., Docker, VMs) facilitates dissemination and coordination. The exemplars collectively emphasize **domain-specific, ad-hoc integration** over commercial frameworks, with **Raspberry Pi** and **single-board computers** serving as edge nodes for real-time data processing."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The **GreenhouseDT** exemplar emphasizes **self-adaptive behavioral models** with fidelity ensured through **prerecorded data** in virtual machine images, allowing for offline validation against historical scenarios. The **Air Quality Management** exemplar employs **runtime visualization, simulation, and prediction** to validate model accuracy, with **AutomationML-based models** enabling structured fidelity checks. Uncertainty sources likely include sensor noise, environmental variability, and model approximation errors, though explicit quantification methods (e.g., Monte Carlo simulations, Bayesian inference) are not detailed. Error detection is implicit in the **ad-hoc digital twin pipelines**, where discrepancies between physical and digital twin states trigger adaptive corrections. The **Microsoft Azure tools** in the Air Quality exemplar suggest cloud-based validation frameworks, while the **ad-hoc approach** in GreenhouseDT relies on manual cross-verification between InfluxDB-stored data and ActiveMQ-mediated updates. Quality assurance processes are not explicitly described but are inferred to involve **data consistency checks** (e.g., sensor calibration, message broker validation) and **model retraining** for improved fidelity."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The **Digital Twin’s Technical Connection** infrastructure relies on a **service-oriented architecture** with the following key components: **MQTT Broker** for lightweight, publish-subscribe messaging between services (e.g., **Trajectory Generator Service**, **Simulation Service**, **Logging Application**). The **network topology** is centralized, with services communicating via the broker and a **TimescaleDB database** acting as a central data repository. **Communication protocols** include MQTT (for inter-service signaling) and direct Python-based interfaces (e.g., **Physical Crane Interface** ↔ **Control Application**). **Bandwidth and latency requirements** are critical for real-time operations; the **Trajectory Generator Service** and **Continuous Validation Service** demand low-latency data flows to ensure trajectory optimization and validation. **Reliability and redundancy mechanisms** are not explicitly detailed, but the **Continuous Validation Service** implies fault detection via threshold breaches. **Security protocols** are not mentioned, though the use of a dedicated **MQTT Broker** and structured data flows suggests controlled access to services."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The **Digital Twin’s Hosting and Deployment** infrastructure is **on-premise**, co-located with the **Physical Crane** system. The **computational requirements** are distributed across six containers: **Database** (TimescaleDB), **Trajectory Generator Service**, **Simulation Service**, **Continuous Validation Service**, **Dashboard** (Grafana), and **MQTT Broker**. **Storage requirements** are handled by the **TimescaleDB database**, which stores time-series data for crane operations, simulations, and validations. **Scalability mechanisms** are not explicitly described, but the modular design (e.g., separate services for trajectory generation, simulation, and validation) allows for potential horizontal scaling of individual components. **Deployment models** are containerized (per C4 Model abstraction) but not specified as cloud/edge/hybrid; the system is tailored for local research use. **Availability/reliability assurance** is implied by the **Continuous Validation Service**, which monitors system performance and alerts operators to deviations, though no explicit redundancy or failover mechanisms are mentioned."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The provided Digital Twin exemplars exhibit **limited horizontal integration** with external systems. **GreenhouseDT** and **Air Quality Management** do not reference **ERP, WMS, or other enterprise systems**, focusing instead on **internal toolchains**:
  - **GreenhouseDT**: Uses **InfluxDB** (data storage), **ActiveMQ** (internal messaging), and **virtual machines** (local deployment) with no external dependencies.
  - **Air Quality Management**: Relies on **Microsoft Azure tools** (cloud-based) for pipeline execution but lacks explicit interoperability standards (e.g., OPC UA, MQTT for external systems). The **Raspberry Pi** sensors interface directly with the digital twin without gateway protocols.
  - **Gantry Crane**: No external system integrations are mentioned; the architecture is **self-contained** with **C4 Model** visualization and **containerized deployment** (likely Python/MQTT-based, inferred from domain context). **No interoperability standards** (e.g., OPC UA, REST APIs) or **enterprise system dependencies** (e.g., SCADA, MES) are documented. The **ad-hoc approach** prioritizes internal cohesion over external extensibility."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin exemplars demonstrate **partial adherence to industry standards**, with the following key observations:
  - **AutomationML**: The Air Quality Management exemplar explicitly uses **AutomationML** for digital twin modeling, a standard for engineering data exchange in automation and control systems (IEC 62714), ensuring interoperability with other engineering tools (e.g., PLCs, SCADA).
  - **Cloud and Data Standards**: Microsoft Azure tools are employed in the Air Quality Management pipeline, though no specific cloud standards (e.g., ISO/IEC 19086 for cloud interoperability) are mentioned. InfluxDB (GreenhouseDT) adheres to **time-series database standards** (e.g., InfluxData’s line protocol), while ActiveMQ uses **JMS (Java Message Service)** for message brokering.
  - **Hardware Standards**: Raspberry Pi devices are used, which comply with **ARM architecture standards** and **Linux-based embedded systems conventions**, though no domain-specific cyber-physical system (CPS) standards (e.g., IEC 62443 for industrial security) are referenced.
  - **Interoperability**: The use of **containerization/virtualization** (e.g., Docker, VM images) aligns with **portability standards** (e.g., Open Container Initiative), but no explicit API or data interchange standards (e.g., OPC UA, FDT/DTM) are mentioned for cross-system communication.
  - **Certification/Compliance**: No formal certification requirements (e.g., ISO 9001, IEC 61508 for safety) or compliance frameworks (e.g., GDPR for data privacy) are documented. The ad-hoc nature of the exemplars suggests a research-focused rather than production-ready compliance posture.
  - **Domain-Specific Standards**: The Air Quality Management exemplar targets **construction engineering**, but no building automation standards (e.g., ASHRAE 62.1 for ventilation) are explicitly cited. Similarly, GreenhouseDT operates in **agricultural IoT**, with no mention of standards like ISO 11783 (ISOBUS) for agricultural machinery."
]
